They say home is where the heart is, but I would proclaim home
is, but I would proclaim home is where the good food resides — which is epitomized by Berkeley’s food scene. Whether you’re meandering about College Avenue, Southside or Downtown Berkeley, there’s a

Downtown Berkeley, there’s a plethora of hotspots and local hidden gems, directly catered to all of you foodies.
For those of you who accessorize with “walking lattes” or “walking boba drinks,” our r
r “walking boba drinks,” our readers recommend Sodoi Coffee Tasting House and Yifang Taiwan Fruit Tea. For those of you desiring to be seen on Taco Tuesday with a Golden State Cider in hand, Tap Haus

State Cider in hand, Tap Haus is the place to be. For those who are struck with a case of the midnight munchies, Berkeley is the home of dessert divinities such as Yogurt Park and tasty Mexican restau

Park and tasty Mexican restaurants such as La Burrita.
Beyond the heavenly offerings of diverse cuisines conveniently located alongside reliable grocery stores, Berkeley features restaurants for shar

features restaurants for sharing meals or conveniently SnackPassing.
In a toast to finding comfort in the bites that make Berkeley taste a little more like home, we present to you the Best of Berkele

ent to you the Best of Berkeley: Food Edition!
- Aimee Han
Eating comically large slices of pizza under the 2 a.m. moonlight on the staggered benches of Artichoke Basille’s Pizza, often shortened to A

’s Pizza, often shortened to Artichokes, is a quintessential UC Berkeley student experience.
With its extensive hours and unique flavors, Artichokes’ pizza is there for campus students — whether they’

ampus students — whether they’re taking a lunch break between classes or stumbling down Southside after a night out on Frat Row.
Though its origins date back to New York City in 2008, Artichokes has b

City in 2008, Artichokes has become just as much of a Southside Berkeley classic. The quick-service restaurant draws crowds of Berkeleyans to the corner of Durant and Bowditch, only increasing in size

ditch, only increasing in size as the night goes on. As the edges of your fresh-out-of-the-oven Margherita, artichoke or vodka sauce slice hang over the rim of the paper plate, your stomach is sure to

plate, your stomach is sure to be satisfied.
Artichokes is the type of restaurant that hallmarks college towns — delicious slices, late-night hours and students making memories in between bites of piz

mories in between bites of pizza.
- Sofia Wallace
Soothing an itch for a sweet treat should be the least of your worries as a busy college student. Thankfully, Berkeley staple Yogurt Park offers more

taple Yogurt Park offers more than just the towering swirls of frozen yogurt that magically never tip over.
Since opening its doors in 1977, this tiny gem located just off Durant Avenue has served as

f Durant Avenue has served as a go-to dessert destination for generations of UC Berkeley students. With a daily rotating selection of flavors — ranging from vibrant fruit blends to classic vanilla — Y

blends to classic vanilla — Yogurt Park proves that frozen yogurt can be both a nostalgic indulgence and a fresh twist on dessert.
What truly sets Yogurt Park apart from other frozen yogurt shops isn
other frozen yogurt shops isn’t just the yogurt itself but its abundance of toppings. You can pile on your favorite mix-ins like cookie dough or cheesecake bites, both throughout the cup and on top,

hroughout the cup and on top, creating a delightful avalanche of flavors that ensure every bite is satisfying.
Whether you’re treating yourself after a tough midterm, fighting late-night cravings foll
hting late-night cravings following a long study session or just in need of a refreshing dessert on a sunny day, Yogurt Park is the perfect spot. Affordable, reliable and always bursting with flavor,

always bursting with flavor, it’s no wonder this place has earned a special place in the hearts of locals and students alike.
- Isabella Merino
Craving a late-night weiner after a drunken night at Ta

er after a drunken night at Tap Haus (2025 Best of Berkeley Bar)? Top Dog, conveniently located on Southside, is only a few storefronts over.
Offering a plethora of options to satisfy your savory crav

ns to satisfy your savory cravings, from the classic frankfurter to the vegetarian wienie, Top Dog is a true Berkeley staple for everyone. If you like toppings on your hot dogs, then you’re in luck. I

t dogs, then you’re in luck. In the small but mighty condiment bar, you can find a variety of mustards, sauerkraut, onion, relish and, of course, ketchup.
Whether it’s a game day or the middle of the

game day or the middle of the night, it’s always rush hour here. While the lines may be long, the wait typically isn’t. Trust me — it’s worth it. And if repping Cal merch is not your thing, you can ac

is not your thing, you can acquire a Top Dog T-shirt or baseball cap for $20.
Either way, Top Dog and all that it stands for is a quintessential embodiment of the Berkeley spirit.
- Andrea Chu-Tam
Lo

ey spirit.
- Andrea Chu-Tam
Located on the lively Durant Avenue, Tap Haus is a two-story adventure that college students of legal drinking age should embark upon during their time at Berkeley. Heavily

heir time at Berkeley. Heavily populated on Tuesdays, Thursdays and during the weekend, this Southside fixture has something for everyone.
Whether it is racking up for a quick game of pool or mingling

quick game of pool or mingling with friends you haven’t seen since freshman year, Tap Haus is by far the best place to socialize once you hit 21.
Drinkwise, a signature boot or green tea shot is highl

oot or green tea shot is highly recommended. Although Tap Haus itself does not serve food, it is conveniently located next to many iconic eateries that are open late for a quick pit stop on your way b

a quick pit stop on your way back home.
If you are looking for a final, close-to-home stop on your bar crawl or if you are just warming up for your night out, Tap Haus is a staple that always merits a

a staple that always merits a visit.
- Emma Solomon
Located by Durant Plaza, Kimchi Garden is a short four-minute walk from campus. For many students, it’s the go-to spot for comfort food — and with

t for comfort food — and with good reason.
With a menu featuring more than 85 food items, Kimchi Garden offers a diverse selection of Korean dishes, ranging from Korean BBQ to soups, stews, rice bowls

BQ to soups, stews, rice bowls and more. However, their kimchi fried rice, tofu soup, beef kimbap, spicy tteokbokki and tonkatsu mayo rice bowl stand out as customer favorites. Open until at least mid

rites. Open until at least midnight seven days a week, their bingsu and tasty drinks also make for a solid late-night sweet treat.
If you’re looking to dine in, the restaurant’s expansive seating area
urant’s expansive seating area and casual ambience make it the prime location to catch up with friends. Or if you’re in need of some quick takeout, options to order ahead online or on kiosks in-store

online or on kiosks in-store make for speedy and convenient service when you’re in a rush.
Be it lunch or dinner, dine in or takeout, Kimchi Garden is sure to have you covered.
- Christina Chang
Nest

overed.
- Christina Chang
Nestled within the busyness of Downtown Berkeley lies Ippudo Ramen, located on 2015 Kalai Bagai Way.
Although Ippudo Ramen serves a multitude of unique variations — per the c
unique variations — per the chef’s personal preference — every bowl consists of a perfect “trifecta of soup, noodles and toppings.” Whether you’re slurping down on the “Shiromaru Classic” featuring t
Shiromaru Classic” featuring thin noodles, its signature “tonkotsu” pork broth and pork belly chashu or the “Akamaru Veggie” composed of a sesame broth, thin noodles and tofu, your taste buds will be

tofu, your taste buds will be dancing with delight.
For those with an affinity for Japanese cuisine, the chefs can prepare perfectly handcrafted noodles with a variety of toppings, such as “tamago,” s

toppings, such as “tamago,” sweet corn and bamboo shoots. Although the menu doesn’t end there, you can also order yummy starters such as chicken “karaage” or pork buns.
Beneath the dimmed lights and

Beneath the dimmed lights and atop the cozy wooden tables, Ippudo Ramen is an underrated find for sharing meals — solidifying bowls of ramen as Japanese “soul food.”
- Aimee Han
Another Asian Ghetto g

mee Han
Another Asian Ghetto gem — Punjabi Dhaba is a family-owned restaurant serving Berkeley’s most coveted Indian food.
You smell the delectable waft of curry as you enter the holy walkway between

nter the holy walkway between Gypsy’s Trattoria Italiana and Sweetheart Cafe, into the bustling nook of Asian Ghetto. Even with the array of food options whispering your name, you find yourself floati

name, you find yourself floating on that aroma of chicken tikka masala.
You scan the seating inside the establishment and around the area and let out a sigh of disappointment as you see no open tables

ment as you see no open tables. How will you enjoy this world class dish with nowhere to sit?
Punjabi Dhaba is one step ahead. Nowhere to sit? No problem, munch on a chicken tikka burrito as you saunt

ken tikka burrito as you saunter down Durant Avenue. Not in the mood for naan? Order yourself some fries tikka masala.
To accommodate for all preferences, cravings and lifestyles, Punjabi Dhaba offers
festyles, Punjabi Dhaba offers both traditional style dishes such as Tandoori chicken paired with veggie samosas and naan, and fusion items like those mentioned above. All Punjabi Dhaba demands is an

l Punjabi Dhaba demands is an adequate spice tolerance.
- Eliana Posin
Four years ago, I walked into Mezzo — a charming, spacious salad and sandwich spot with ample seating where prices range from $4

ng where prices range from $4 to $16 — and ordered for the first time. Nothing could have prepared me for what would happen next.
Before my eyes a bowl of greens, carrots, chickpeas, sprouts, kidney b

, chickpeas, sprouts, kidney beans and cherry tomatoes suddenly materialized, topped with a hard-boiled egg and half an avocado. It was comically large, practically overflowing. Baffled, I ate half an

lowing. Baffled, I ate half and took half to-go, pondering if my next order would be the chicken salad atop honey wheat bread or a BLTA — bacon, lettuce, tomato, avocado — on sea salt sourdough. The p

— on sea salt sourdough. The possibilities were and are endless.
Throughout college, relationships have ebbed and flowed, midterms have neared and passed and the months have warmed and cooled, but a c

ave warmed and cooled, but a constant has been the abundance of a Mezzo sandwich and salad. I’ve enjoyed Mezzo in the sun-dappled patio behind the restaurant, in my Friday night Christian fellowship g

y night Christian fellowship group and now in my apartment on Haste Street.
Through generous portions, fresh ingredients and its cheerfully humming atmosphere, Mezzo affirms itself as a gathering spac

rms itself as a gathering space uniquely committed to both quality and quantity.
- Karissa Ho
Established in 2012 and tucked away in an alley by Unit 3, Sodoi Coffee Tasting House is a crowd favorite

ing House is a crowd favorite among students in search for their caffeine fix.

ns perfectly with the values of environmentally conscious eaters.
